seoTitle: How to Add a Progress Bar in ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to set up automatic or manual progress bars in ClickUp using custom fields to visualize task and project completion.
intro: Seeing task completion at a glance makes it easier to spot what's stalled without opening every card individually. This guide covers adding a Progress custom field in ClickUp, choosing between automatic tracking based on subtasks and checklists or manual percentage entry, and displaying the resulting bar across List, Table, and Kanban views.

steps.heading: Add a Progress custom field
steps.body: Open your target list and click the '+' column icon, then search the fields menu for Progress and name your new column.

steps.heading: Choose Auto or Manual mode
steps.body: Select Progress (Auto) to calculate completion based on subtasks and checklists, or Progress (Manual) to let team members enter a percentage directly.

steps.heading: Configure fallback rules
steps.body: Adjust settings to determine how tasks without any subtasks or checklist items should display, whether that's 0% or 100%.

steps.heading: Create the field
steps.body: Click Create to inject the visual progress bar into your current view layout.

steps.heading: Test the auto-progress bar
steps.body: Check off items on a task's checklist and watch the bar update in real time to confirm it's calculating correctly.

steps.heading: Add calculation footers
steps.body: Turn on the calculation summary header at the bottom of the column to track the overall average progress across your list.

faq.question: What's the difference between Auto and Manual progress?
faq.answer: Auto progress calculates completion automatically based on subtasks and checklists, while Manual progress lets a team member set the percentage themselves.

faq.question: Can I hide the progress field from external guests?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust column permissions to hide progress data from guests while keeping it visible to internal team members.

faq.question: Does the progress bar update in every view?
faq.answer: Yes, once added as a custom field, it displays consistently across List, Table, and Kanban views.

faq.question: What happens to progress on a task with no subtasks?
faq.answer: You can configure fallback settings to decide whether tasks without subtasks show as 0% or 100% complete.
